display_name: Monochrome Terminal — P31 green (Apple II / VT100 class)

source.notes: Monochrome CRT — single P31 phosphor, no shadow mask. Tube geometry approximates typical 12" Apple II / Commodore PET / VT100 terminal hardware of the era. dot_pitch_mm is N/A for monochrome (single phosphor layer).

P31 = ZnS:Cu or ZnS:Cu,Ag — yellowish-green, ~525 nm peak, short persistence 0.01-1 ms (EIA TEP-116 range; typical ~38 µs). CIE 1931 xy (0.226, 0.555) per Wikipedia phosphor table — corrected from previous (0.260, 0.530) which was an averaged value. Used in oscilloscopes and computer monochrome monitors (Apple Monitor III, VT100 green variants). Note: IBM 5151 is actually P39 high-persistence green, not P31 — but P31 is the dominant green-terminal phosphor of the era.
